Title: 'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
Subhead: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

Title: Xi, at the center of global affairs, keeps Putin waiting on the Power of Siberia 2 pipeline
Israel faces European backlash over Gaza flotilla detentions
Who is Emmanuel Moulin, the new governor of France's central bank?
Subhead: Parliament approved the appointment of the former Elysée chief of staff to head the Banque de France on Wednesday, May 20. Opponents criticized his closeness to both Nicolas Sarkozy and Emmanuel Macron.

Title: Hungarian PM Magyar signals break with Orban-era diplomacy during Poland visit
Subhead: For his first official trip abroad, Hungary's new prime minister, Peter Magyar, chose Poland, clearly showing a desire to rekindle diplomatic relations with Donald Tusk's pro-European government. The Hungarian leader is set to visit Vienna on Thursday, and then Brussels on Sunday and Monday.

Title: Sweden and France become privileged defense partners
Subhead: Stockholm announced, on May 19, that it wanted to acquire four defense and intervention frigates from the French company Naval Group. The order shows Sweden's growing strategic ties with Paris, facilitated by its NATO accession.

Title: 'Gaza has become a diplomatic, political and military black hole'
Subhead: Donald Trump's promises for the territory, devastated after two years of war, have continued to be proven wrong in the months following the ceasefire with Israel, writes Gilles Paris, columnist for Le Monde, in his column.

'France's president-PM diarchy is shaping the strategy of many 2027 hopefuls'
Subhead: The role of prime minister has regained central importance since 2022 and the end of outright majorities in the Assemblée Nationale, observes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

Title: Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
Subhead: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

Title: 'A more arbitrary and extreme regime': In the West Bank, Israel toughens death penalty law for Palestinians
Subhead: A coalition of Israeli and Palestinian non-profit organizations has called for the cancellation of the 'military order' that brought the measure into force, expanding the crimes punishable by death and reducing the means of defense.
